items.title: The global M&A boom is rolling into 2026 as AI sparks deal frenzy — but cash is getting tight
items.description: Markets are betting that the global M&A surge has not yet finished, as Wall Street recovered its appetite for large-scale financings.

items.title: Wednesday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
items.description: Software stocks bounced after the latest bout of selling, lifting the major averages to a winning session Tuesday.

items.title: The Anti-AI Trade Is Red Hot. Here’s What’s Beating Artificial- Intelligence Stocks.
items.description: Investors fleeing hard-hit software companies are taking comfort in stocks that might not get disrupted as much by the artificial-intelligence revolution.  Analysts at Goldman Sachs noted in a report Tuesday that a basket of European companies it considers capital-intensive has outperformed capital-light stocks—in industries such as media, retail, business services, and software—by 35% since 2025.  Airbus BMW Volkswagen Nestle Diageo  and ASML were some of the companies in Goldman’s capital-intensive basket.

items.title: Governments are rushing to hoard critical minerals as the 'resource nationalism' era arrives
items.description: A new race to secure critical minerals is unfolding across the global economy.

items.title: Citigroup Agrees to Sell 24% Banamex Stake, Moves Closer to IPO Plan
items.description: C agrees to sell 24% of Banamex to institutional investors, edging closer to a potential IPO for its Mexican banking unit.
